properties.name: disableQuickPerf
properties.description: When true, completely disables QuickPerf for the current test execution. Performance annotations (@ExpectSelect, @ProfileJvm, ...) become no-ops. Resolved from (in order) the disableQuickPerf JVM system property, @SpringBootTest(properties = ...), application.properties, and application.yml. Read at test time.

properties.name: limitQuickPerfSqlInfoOnConsole
properties.description: When true, restricts the verbosity of QuickPerf's SQL diagnostic output (executed queries, parameters, JDBC suggestions) printed to the test console. Resolved from -DlimitQuickPerfSqlInfoOnConsole, @SpringBootTest(properties = ...), application.properties, or application.yml.

properties.name: limitQuickPerfJvmInfoOnConsole
properties.description: When true, restricts the verbosity of QuickPerf's JVM-profiling diagnostic output (allocations, GC, exceptions, JVM details) printed to the test console. Resolved from -DlimitQuickPerfJvmInfoOnConsole, @SpringBootTest(properties = ...), application.properties, or application.yml.

properties.name: quickperf.classPathJarThreshold
properties.description: Maximum length, in characters, of the java.class.path system property that QuickPerf will pass as-is to a forked JVM (used by annotations such as @HeapSize, @Xmx that require a separate JVM). When the actual classpath length exceeds this threshold, QuickPerf packages the entries into a temporary manifest-only JAR with a Class-Path attribute, side-stepping operating-system command-line length limits. Default: 2048. Today this property must be set via the -Dquickperf.classPathJarThreshold=... JVM system property; resolution from application.properties, application.yml, or @SpringBootTest(properties = ...) is not yet wired.
